# UX Writing Reference

Great interfaces sound confident, concise, and specific.

## Principles

- Make every word earn its place.
- Label what the user can do, not what the system feels.
- Reduce repetition.

## Do

- use direct, concrete verbs
- write labels that match user intent
- use helper text only when it reduces friction
- make empty states teach the next step
- make error text actionable and calm

## Avoid

- repeating headings in the paragraph below them
- vague filler like “manage everything in one place”
- generic empty states like “No items found” without guidance
- overexplaining what the user can already see
